Now there arose a new king over {{c1::Egypt}}, who knew not Joseph.

And he said unto his people: ‘Behold, the people of the children of {{c1::Israel}} are too many and too mighty for us;

come, let us deal {{c1::wisely}} with them, lest they multiply, and it come to pass, that, when there befalleth us any war, they also join themselves unto our enemies, and fight against us, and get them up out of the land.’

And the king of {{c1::Egypt}} spoke to the Hebrew midwives, of whom the name of the one was Shiphrah, and the name of the other Puah;

and he said: ‘When ye do the office of a midwife to the {{c1::Hebrew}} women, ye shall look upon the birthstool: if it be a son, then ye shall kill him; but if it be a daughter, then she shall live.’
and he said: ‘When ye do the {{c1::office}} of a midwife to the Hebrew women, ye shall look upon the birthstool: if it be a son, then ye shall kill him; but if it be a daughter, then she shall live.’
But the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of {{c1::Egypt}} commanded them, but saved the men-children alive.

And the king of Egypt called for the midwives, and said unto them: ‘{{c1::Why}} have ye done this thing, and have saved the men-children alive?’
And the midwives said unto {{c1::Pharaoh}}: ‘Because the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian women; for they are lively, and are delivered ere the midwife come unto them.’
And the midwives said unto Pharaoh: ‘{{c1::Because}} the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian women; for they are lively, and are delivered ere the midwife come unto them.’
And God dealt well with the {{c1::midwives}}; and the people multiplied, and waxed very mighty.

And when she could not {{c1::longer}} hide him, she took for him an ark of bulrushes, and daubed it with slime and with pitch; and she put the child therein, and laid it in the flags by the river’s brink.

And his {{c1::sister}} stood afar off, to know what would be done to him.
And the daughter of {{c1::Pharaoh}} came down to bathe in the river; and her maidens walked along by the river-side; and she saw the ark among the flags, and sent her handmaid to fetch it.
And the {{c1::daughter}} of Pharaoh came down to bathe in the river; and her maidens walked along by the river-side; and she saw the ark among the flags, and sent her handmaid to fetch it.
And she opened it, and saw it, even the child; and behold a boy that wept. And she had compassion on him, and said: ‘{{c1::This}} is one of the Hebrews’children.’

And the child grew, and she brought him unto {{c1::Pharaoh’s}} daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name Moses, and said: ‘Because I drew him out of the water.’
And the child grew, and she brought him unto Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name {{c1::Moses}}, and said: ‘Because I drew him out of the water.’
And it came to pass in those days, when {{c1::Moses}} was grown up, that he went out unto his brethren, and looked on their burdens; and he saw an Egyptian smiting a Hebrew, one of his brethren.
And it came to pass in those days, when Moses was grown up, that he went out unto his brethren, and looked on their burdens; and he saw an {{c1::Egyptian}} smiting a Hebrew, one of his brethren.

And he went out the second day, and, behold, two men of the {{c1::Hebrews}} were striving together; and he said to him that did the wrong: ‘Wherefore smitest thou thy fellow?’

And he said: ‘Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us? thinkest thou to kill me, as thou didst kill the {{c1::Egyptian}}?’ And Moses feared, and said: ‘Surely the thing is known.’
Now when {{c1::Pharaoh}} heard this thing, he sought to slay Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and dwelt in the land of Midian; and he sat down by a well.

And she bore a son, and he called his name Gershom; for he said: ‘{{c1::I}} have been a stranger in a strange land.’

And it came to pass in the course of those many days that the king of {{c1::Egypt}} died; and the children of Israel sighed by reason of the bondage, and they cried, and their cry came up unto God by reason of the bondage.

And He said: ‘{{c1::Draw}} not nigh hither; put off thy shoes from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground.’

and {{c1::I}} am come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land unto a good land and a large, unto a land flowing with milk and honey; unto the place of the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

Go, and gather the elders of {{c1::Israel}} together, and say unto them: The LORD, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, hath appeared unto me, saying: I have surely remembered you, and seen that which is done to you in Egypt.

And {{c1::I}} have said: I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt unto the land of the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, unto a land flowing with milk and honey.

And now let us go, we pray thee, three days’journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God.

that they may believe that the LORD, the God of their fathers, the God of {{c1::Abraham}}, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, hath appeared unto thee.’
that they may believe that the LORD, the God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of {{c1::Isaac}}, and the God of Jacob, hath appeared unto thee.’

And {{c1::Moses}} said unto the LORD: ‘Oh Lord, I am not a man of words, neither heretofore, nor since Thou hast spoken unto Thy servant; for I am slow of speech, and of a slow tongue.’
